meta.title: Jonatan García Ripollés — Agentic Developer & AI Expert
meta.description: Industrial engineer, agentic developer and founder of NeoNexAI Agency. I design and run AI systems that actually work: voice agents, automations and custom software in production.

hero.name: Jonatan García Ripollés
hero.roles: Industrial Engineer · Agentic Developer · AI Expert · CEO & Founder of NeoNexAI AGENCY
hero.intro: I design and run AI systems that actually work: custom professional websites, voice agents, phone assistants and automations for companies, SMBs and small businesses that want to automate their day-to-day.

manifesto.line: I don't sell AI magic — I sell systems you can audit, verify and repeat. If it can't survive a real client, it doesn't leave my machine.

now.label: Now
now.title: I founded NeoNexAI Agency.
now.body: In 2026 I started my own AI agency for companies and SMBs. Everything below is in production, with real clients — not weekend projects.
now.focus: Shipping the point-of-sale module for restaurants and refining how my coding agents verify their own work before handing it back to me.

projects.label: Projects in production
projects.title: What I've built
projects.lead: Every project is live and verifiable. The proof is that it works with clients, not the promise.

p1.name: neonexai.com
p1.desc: Most AI agency landings look the same. Mine had to prove what I sell, not just describe it — so I designed, built and run it myself, end to end.
p1.b2: Trilingual, technical SEO and custom conversion analytics

p2.name: Client portal
p2.desc: My clients were hiring several different services with nowhere to see them together. I built a multi-tenant app where each one manages their own from a single place.
p2.b1: Bookings, orders and kitchen in real time, per venue
p2.b2: AI academy, deliverables and billing built in

p3.name: Open-source projects
p3.desc: When a client's problem didn't have a tool that solved it well, I built one and shipped it in the open — now they use it daily.
p3.b1: Custom Claude connectors for an architecture studio: quotes, CAD and time tracking (PyPI + npm, with CI)
p3.b2: A bot that keeps the machine active 24/7 to mine Grass points without having to sit in front of it

p4.name: Custom premium websites
p4.desc: Generic templates don't hold up for a real business. I design every website from scratch, per client — restaurants with bookings and QR menus, and any other service sector.
p4.b1: Restaurants: automatic bookings, digital QR menu and WhatsApp confirmation
p4.b2: Bespoke design per client — no templates, no third-party lock-in

p5.name: Phone voice agent
p5.desc: The phone was still the bottleneck for my restaurant clients. I built a voice agent that answers it on its own, with no shifts and no interruptions for the team.
p5.b1: Takes bookings automatically and matches them to live capacity
p5.b2: Handles customers and screens potential-lead calls, 24/7

now.desc: From zero to production systems with clients on the books, alongside my job at IGEX. I design the architecture, write the code and run the infrastructure.

igex.desc: Energy efficiency: audits, certifications and complex thermal systems. I've planned and coordinated 10+ simultaneous projects, with budgets of over €500,000 in a single project. This is where it all started: frustrated by the hours lost to repetitive tasks, I automated document management with Python and n8n and a 2-hour task dropped to 5 minutes. That first win hooked me — I haven't stopped building AI agents and redesigning processes so the machine takes the tedious part and people keep the judgment.

master.desc: Systems optimization, process improvement (Lean, Six Sigma), energy management and industrial sustainability.

degree.desc: Technical foundations: thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, thermal systems, electricity, materials and engineering projects.

resto.desc: Four years of service under real pressure, awarded the Repsol Guide Solete. Life comes full circle: its sister restaurant, El Nou Temple, is now a client of my agency.

ai.title: AI and agents
ai.items: Claude Code · Multi-agent architectures · MCP (custom connectors) · RAG · Context engineering · Loop Engineering · Guardrails · VAPI (voice) · LLM orchestration · Prompt engineering

dev.title: Development and automation
dev.items: Python · TypeScript · Next.js and React · Docker · n8n · PostgreSQL / Supabase · REST APIs · Git · 3D web · Linux / VPS

eng.title: Engineering and energy
eng.items: Thermal systems and HVAC · Renewables (solar, aerothermal, biomass) · Energy audits · ISO 50001 · CYPE · TESTO · AISLAM · Project management (Lean, Six Sigma)

hitl.title: Human in the loop + Loop Engineering
hitl.body: AI does the heavy lifting; I audit, iterate and sign off. I work with Loop Engineering: I design the context and instructions so coding agents extend their own capabilities, self-evaluate what they produce, verify and iterate on their own, and hand me back a result that's already been cleaned up. I never promise what the tech can't deliver — honesty is the argument.

discipline.title: High-performance discipline
discipline.body: I built a personal ecosystem of training, nutrition and rest — not as a new-year resolution, but as a pillar and foundation of my everyday. That strict consistency is the same one I apply to every project: systems over impulses, repetition over luck. Resilience and constant improvement are the engine behind every architecture I orchestrate.

curiosity.title: Curiosity underneath
curiosity.body: I study physics and quantum physics out of genuine curiosity and for the ambition of its possible fusion with AI in the future. Without chasing credentials: it's an exploration that gives me tools for abstract, creative analysis to deconstruct technical problems from angles no AI course teaches.

uses.label: /uses
uses.title: What I use
uses.lead: Not the stack I sell — what I actually keep open on my own desk every day.

i1.desc: My editor and terminal at once; I supervise, I don't type every line.

i2.desc: One entry point to several models, with fallback if one goes down.

i3.desc: The default framework for any website or portal I build.

i4.desc: Database and auth on my own VPS, not a third party's cloud.

i5.desc: I deploy every project in containers, with no PaaS lock-in.

i6.desc: Everything running on the VPS runs in a container, reproducibly.

i7.desc: Automations that don't need a full AI agent.

i8.desc: The voice engine behind the agents that answer the phone.

i9.desc: Every change is versioned and reversible.

i10.desc: I'd rather control the infrastructure than rent it by request.

cta.label: Next step
cta.title: Got a business and want AI to work for you?
cta.body: That's exactly what we do at NeoNexAI. Tell me what you need and I'll honestly tell you whether it fits — and how.
